summary: Implement minimal campaign AI target, wait, withdraw, and objective-change reasons
acceptance_criteria: Minimal campaign AI evaluates campaign objectives, muster readiness, grain supply, route capacity, monsoon timing, siege state, and postwar risk using the same query/command surface available to the player.
AI can choose no action/wait, start or change target, continue, reinforce, withdraw, or cancel a campaign and emits visible primary reasons for each decision.
AI command submission uses the same GameCommand payloads as player actions and does not mutate authoritative WorldState outside the simulation command application path.
AI traces are deterministic, serializable, bounded in size, and suitable for later UI and validation consumption.
The task does not implement full diplomacy AI, multi-year grand strategy, personality simulation, arbitrary-code scripting, telemetry, or server/multiplayer logic.
required_tests: Scenario tests cover AI waiting for supply/season, target change, reinforcement, withdrawal, and choosing no action with visible reasons.
Property/invariant tests cover player/AI command parity, deterministic AI trace ordering, bounded trace size, and no hidden authoritative mutation.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-CAMPAIGN-AI-001

simulation_engineer.responsibility: primary minimal M4 campaign AI writer

systems_architect.responsibility: architecture reviewer for minimal M4 campaign AI

notes: Keep AI minimal and explanatory; do not build a general strategic-planning manager.
If AI needs missing reason fields, route back to the relevant M4 sim task rather than duplicating hidden formulas.

Explicit exclusions for M4 remain: manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product or architecture changes.
